perturbation.original_text: ●All revenue sharing will be reconciled monthly and funds distributed by the 20th of each month by wire transfer for all net collected revenues for the previous month (net of returns and refunds, if any).
perturbation.changed_text: ●All revenue sharing will be reconciled monthly and funds distributed by the 10th of each month by wire transfer for all net collected revenues for the previous month (net of returns and refunds, if any).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment distribution date. Originally, revenue sharing funds were to be distributed by the 20th of each month. The modification changes this to the 10th of each month. This directly conflicts with the later clause which mentions the 20th as the date of reconcile, making it contradictory about when funds are disbursed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ●The iMedicor bookkeeping system will detail the payment option prior to releasing any funds or commitment of stock in order for USA MCO to determine how payment is to be reconciled. It is anticipated that said report will be available by the 15th of the month for previous activity, returned to iMedicor by the 19th in order to effect a timely disbursement of funds and or stock ●It is understood that the issuance of stock certificates can take up to four weeks but the issue date will be the 20th of the month of reconcile.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the date for revenue sharing distribution from the 20th to the 10th of each month. The contradicted text states that the issue date will be the 20th of the month. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the disbursement timeline, indicating a strong in-text contradiction, therefore 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date of this Agreement and shall remain in effect for a period of 5 years ("Initial Term"). Thereafter, this Agreement shall be renewed automatically on a five (5) year basis, unless one party notifies the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement at least sixty 90 days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or then current renewal term, as applicable, or unless a new Agreement is signed between the USA MCO and IMedicor which will then invalidate this Agreement. In such an instance, all compensation will continue for a extended period of five years from the date of termination for all subscriptions tagged as USA MCO.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date of this Agreement and shall remain in effect for a period of 5 years ("Initial Term"). Thereafter, this Agreement shall be renewed automatically on a five (5) year basis, unless one party notifies the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement at least thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or then current renewal term, as applicable, or unless a new Agreement is signed between the USA MCO and IMedicor which will then invalidate this Agreement. In such an instance, all compensation will continue for a extended period of one year from the date of termination for all subscriptions tagged as USA MCO.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction related to the termination notice period and continuation of compensation post-termination. The original text specifies a ninety (90) day notice for termination and a five-year continuation of compensation. The modified text changes this to a thirty (30) day notice and a one-year continuation of compensation. This causes contradiction and enforceability regarding the notice period and compensation continuation post termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Termination Without Cause. Both parties may terminate this Agreement upon ninety ("90") days written notice to the other party at the address stated in this Agreement as per section 3 above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states a 90-day termination notice, aligning with the contradicted text. The changed text reduces this to 30 days, directly conflicting with the contradicted text's requirement of 90 days written notice for termination, creating an inconsistency as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: The Warrants will be issued as a non-cash-transaction upon exercise of the Warrant, and, USA MCO will have up to five years from the date of issuance to exe4cute. USA MCO will also have the option to purchase the Warrant for the face value if it deemed the purchase option to be more favorable to the cashless transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Warrants will be issued as a non-cash-transaction upon exercise of the Warrant, and, USA MCO will have up to one year from the date of issuance to exe4cute. USA MCO will also have the option to purchase the Warrant for double the face value regardless if it deemed the purchase option to be more favorable to the cashless transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction related to the term and price of warrants. Originally, USA MCO had five years to exercise the warrant, and the purchase option was at face value if deemed more favorable. Now, USA MCO has only one year to execute, and the option to purchase is at double the face value, regardless. This change is contradictory in the term and the price for purchase.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date of this Agreement and shall remain in effect for a period of 5 years ("Initial Term"). Thereafter, this Agreement shall be renewed automatically on a five (5) year basis, unless one party notifies the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement at least sixty 90 days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or then current renewal term, as applicable, or unless a new Agreement is signed between the USA MCO and IMedicor which will then invalidate this Agreement. In such an instance, all compensation will continue for a extended period of five years from the date of termination for all subscriptions tagged as USA MCO.
perturbation.justification: The changed text shortens the warrant execution period from five years to one year and alters the purchase price. The contradicted text states the agreement remains in effect for five years. This creates a contradiction because the warrant terms, as modified, may expire before the agreement's initial term concludes. This directly contradicts the expectation that the warrant, linked to the agreement, would align with the agreement's duration, creating a conflict regarding the warrant's term and potentially its value within the overall agreement.
